2604.16171.title: JumpLoRA: Sparse Adapters for Continual Learning in Large Language Models
2604.16171.paper_summary: Adapter-based methods have become a cost-effective approach to continual learning (CL) for Large Language Models (LLMs), by sequentially learning a low-rank update matrix for each task. To mitigate catastrophic forgetting, state-of-the-art approaches impose constraints on new adapters with respect to the previous ones, by targeting either subspace or coordinate-wise interference. In this paper, we propose JumpLoRA, a novel framework to adaptively induce sparsity in the Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) blocks through the use of JumpReLU gating. The method achieves dynamic parameter isolation, which helps prevent task interference. We demonstrate that our method is highly modular and compatible with LoRA-based CL approaches. Specifically, it significantly boosts the performance of IncLoRA and outperforms the leading state-of-the-art CL method, ELLA.
2604.16171.paper_summary_zh: 適配器基礎的方法已成為大型語言模型（LLMs）持續學習（CL）的成本效益高的途徑，通過為每個任務順序學習低秩更新矩陣。為了減輕災難性遺忘，最先進的方法對新適配器施加了相對於先前適配器的約束，目標是針對子空間或坐標方向的干擾。在本文中，我們提出了JumpLoRA，一個新穎的框架，通過使用JumpReLU閘來自適應地引入低秩適配（LoRA）區塊中的稀疏性。該方法實現了動態參數隔離，有助於防止任務干擾。我們證明了我們的方法具有高度模組化並與基於LoRA的CL方法兼容。具體而言，它顯著提高了IncLoRA的性能，並超越了領先的最先進CL方法ELLA。
2604.16171.author: Alexandra Dragomir et.al.
2604.16171.authors: Alexandra Dragomir, Ioana Pintilie, Antonio Barbalau, Marius Dragoi, Florin Brad, Cristian Daniel Paduraru, Alexandru Tifrea, Elena Burceanu, Radu Tudor Ionescu
